item.title: Hyperscalers and the equity tap: more to come
item.description: Plus, corporate culture and returns

item.title: Ebola outbreak stirs familiar grim sense of foreboding
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain is a reminder that serious health threats can escalate from under-the-radar pathogens

item.title: Swedish business bristles over EU interventions
item.description: Lawyers and financiers complain that policymakers could be undermining the success of the country’s model

item.title: The UK is stuck in a rollercoaster queue
item.description: British theme parks are a fitting symbol of the country’s supply-side economic woes

item.title: A guide for the perplexed on AI
item.description: Should we think of this technology as boon, bane or bubble?

item.title: The AI boom gives 1990s IT brands a second crack at youth
item.description: Top-line growth is exciting Wall Street even though the groups are just one link in a much larger AI-related domino rally

item.title: How can we persuade our daughter to have a prenup?
item.description: She is getting married this summer but stands to inherit a large amount of money from us

item.title: Index investing will evolve with mega IPOs
item.description: New offerings will be a test but markets ultimately absorb new supply and determine prices over time

item.title: Why ultra-luxury cruises are at risk of running aground
item.description: The core principles of luxury can lose their efficacy on the high seas

item.title: Cuba’s unavoidable reckoning
item.description: US pressure is of dubious morality, but Havana has little choice but to do a deal

item.title: The China-US tech truce is fragile
item.description: A new wave of supply chain conflict is brewing

item.title: Wise money laundering probe hits at fintech’s fundamental flaw
item.description: Investigation comes at a particularly bad time given its recent transatlantic stock market move

item.title: Why young America is trending socialist
item.description: Democrats need to show they can deliver for a disaffected Gen Z

item.title: Google’s $80bn equity raise adds to that giant AI sucking sound
item.description: Search engine’s move is a good example of how artificial intelligence has made big numbers all but meaningless

item.title: Canada’s Maple revolutionaries in pensions feel the strain
item.description: Lacklustre private equity returns weigh on recent performance of a sector leader and others

item.title: IPO boom, market doom?
item.description: Plus: not now, Godzilla

item.title: America’s AI boom is carrying more than investors admit
item.description: The economy, the profit cycle and the market narrative are all leaning on the same narrow pillar

item.title: How did Spain’s unemployment rate converge with Finland’s?
item.description: Migration policy and labour market reforms are affecting joblessness — but in very different ways

item.title: Blair has shone a spotlight on Starmer’s growth delusion
item.description: Labour’s commitment to fixing the sluggish economy comes with a dizzying number of caveats

item.title: China’s housing market could have further to fall
item.description: To make real estate attractive for investors, prices must go lower

item.title: SoftBank data centre offers France AI sovereignty with strings
item.description: The plans are undoubtedly grand but it’s impossible to tell what capacity will be required when the 2030s kick off

item.title: EasyJet may be budget, but an acquirer won’t get it cheap
item.description: Nothing about buying the orange airline would be straightforward

item.title: Myanmar: the forgotten war
item.description: The effort to prove Aung San Suu Kyi’s wellbeing can revive broader pressure on the military

item.title: America at 250 — and the war it forgot
item.description: Native American nations were in a strong position before the 1794 Battle of Fallen Timbers

item.title: Putin could pay a personal price for failure in Ukraine
item.description: History suggests that military defeat often leads to radical political change in Moscow
